Name: Bulbasaur
Flavor-Text: For some time after its birth, it grows by gaining nourishment from the seed on its back.

Name: Ivysaur
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: When the bud on its back starts swelling, a sweet aroma wafts to indicate the flower’s coming bloom.

Name: Venusaur
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: After a rainy day, the flower on its back smells stronger. The scent attracts other Pokémon.

Name: Scyther
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The sharp scythes on its forearms become increasingly sharp by cutting through hard objects.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Your opponent puts the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: It binds itself to trees on marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It basically does not fight. With cells similar to those of plants, it can perform photosynthesis.

Name: Dwebble
Flavor-Text: It makes a hole in a suitable rock. If that rock breaks, the Pokémon remains agitated until it locates a replacement.

Name: Crustle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It possesses legs of enormous strength, enabling it to carry heavy slabs for many days, even when crossing arid land.

Name: Karrablast
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

Name: Shelmet
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid.

Name: Accelgor
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
Flavor-Text: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane.

Name: Flareon
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
You may discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Inhaled air is heated in the flame sac in its body to an intense fire over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Entei-EX
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Torchic
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees F.

Name: Torchic
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees F.

Name: Combusken
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its kicking mastery lets it loose 10 kicks per second. It emits sharp cries to intimidate foes.

Name: Blaziken
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Flames spout from its wrists, enveloping its knuckles. Its punches scorch it foes.

Name: Torkoal
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Durant, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Using their very hot, flame-covered tongues, they burn through Durant’s steel bodies and consume their insides.

Name: Larvesta
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

Name: Larvesta
Effects: This Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: The base of volcanoes is where they make their homes. They shoot fire from their five horns to repel attacking enemies.

Name: Volcarona
Ability-Effect: Put 4 damage counters instead of 2 on your opponent’s Burned Pokémon between turns.
Effects: You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: A sea of fire engulfs the surroundings of their battles, since they use their six wings to scatter their ember scales.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Both this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Although slow, it is skilled at fishing with its tail. It does not feel pain if its tail is bitten.

Name: Slowbro
Ability-Effect: If you have 2, 4, or 6 Prize Cards left, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Though usually dim witted, it seems to become inspired if the Shellder on its tail bites down.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its cell composition is similar to water molecules. As a result, it can melt away into water.

Name: Kyogre-EX
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Piplup
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

Name: Prinplup
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Because every Prinplup considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group.

Name: Empoleon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: If anyone were to hurt its pride, it would slash them with wings that can cleave through an ice floe.

Name: Glaceon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: By controlling its body heat, it can freeze the atmosphere around it to make a diamond-dust flurry.

Name: Tympole
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They warn others of danger by vibrating their cheeks to create a high-pitched sound.

Name: Palpitoad
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to capture prey.

Name: Vanillite
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas surrounding them.

Name: Vanillish
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

Name: Ducklett
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food—peat moss.

Name: Swanna
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: You may discard a [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: If agitated, it uses electricity to straighten out its fur and launch it in small bunches.

Name: Raikou-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Discard all [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Plusle
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If Minun is on your Bench, draw 4 more cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

Name: Minun
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

Name: Joltik
Flavor-Text: They attach themselves to large-bodied Pokémon and absorb static electricity, which they store in an electric pouch.

Name: Joltik
Flavor-Text: They attach themselves to large-bodied Pokémon and absorb static electricity, which they store in an electric pouch.

Name: Galvantula
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
If the Defending Pokémon has no Retreat Cost, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They employ an electrically charged web to trap their prey. While it is immobilized by shock, they leisurely consume it.

Name: Tynamo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon move in schools. They have an electricity-generating organ, so they discharge electricity if in danger.

Name: Tynamo
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon move in schools. They have an electricity-generating organ, so they discharge electricity if in danger.

Name: Eelektrik
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

Name: Eelektross
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: With their sucker mouths, they suck in prey. Then they use their fangs to shock the prey with electricity.

Name: Espeon
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: Its fur is so sensitive, it can sense minute shifts in the air and predict the weather.

Name: Slowking
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Being bitten by Shellder gave it intelligence comparable to that of award-winning scientists.

Name: Woobat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Suction from its nostrils enables it to stick to cave walls during sleep. It leaves a heart-shaped mark behind.

Name: Yamask
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a card at random from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

Name: Cofagrigus
Effects: Discard as many Pokémon Tool cards as you like from your hand. This attack does 40 damage times the number of cards you discarded.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

Name: Aerodactyl
Ability-Effect: Your Pokémon’s attacks do 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that roamed the skies in the dinosaur era. Its teeth are like saw blades.

Name: Groudon-EX
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If the Defending Pokémon already has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 40 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drilbur
Flavor-Text: It makes its way swiftly through the soil by putting both claws together and rotating at high speed.

Name: Excadrill
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: More than 300 feet below the surface, they build mazelike nests. Their activity can be destructive to subway tunnels.

Name: Excadrill
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It can help in tunnel construction. Its drill has evolved into steel strong enough to bore through iron plates.

Name: Timburr
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon appear at building sites and help out with construction. They always carry squared logs.

Name: Gurdurr
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They strengthen their bodies by carrying steel beams. They show off their big muscles to their friends.

Name: Umbreon
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: When exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

Name: Umbreon
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Put 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

Name: Darkrai-EX
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [D] Energy attached to it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sandile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

Name: Krokorok
Flavor-Text: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

Name: Krookodile
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Does 40 damage times the number of Prize cards you have taken.
Flavor-Text: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

Name: Scraggy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of [D] Pokémon you have in play.
Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 10 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey.

Name: Vullaby
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

Name: Escavalier
Effects: Before doing damage, discard a Pokémon Tool card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
Flavor-Text: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

Name: Klink
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Interlocking two bodies and spinning around generates the energy they need to live.

Name: Klang
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Spinning minigears are rotated at high speed and repeatedly fired away. It is dangerous if the gears don’t return.

Name: Klinklang
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: The gear with the red core is rotated at high speed for a rapid energy charge.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: They fight at Bisharp’s command. They cling to their prey and inflict damage by sinking their blades into it.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey.

Name: Chansey
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A kindly Pokémon that lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A kindly Pokémon that lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

Name: Blissey
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, heal 30 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Because its genetic makeup is irregular, it quickly changes its form due to a variety of causes.

Name: Eevee
Flavor-Text: Because its genetic makeup is irregular, it quickly changes its form due to a variety of causes.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its tongue is just like a human’s. As a result, it can cleverly mimic human speech.

Name: Lillipup
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The long hair around its face provides an amazing radar that lets it sense subtle changes in its surroundings.

Name: Herdier
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a black, cape-like fur that is very hard and decreases the amount of damage it receives.

Name: Stoutland
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

Name: Haxorus
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Their sturdy tusks will stay sharp even if used to cut steel beams. These Pokémon are covered in hard armor.

Name: Tornadus-EX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cheren
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Dark Claw
Description: If this card is attached to a [D] Pokémon, each of the attacks of that Pokémon does 20 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Dark Patch
Description: Attach a basic [D] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched [D] Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Enhanced Hammer
Description: Discard a Special Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Hooligans Jim & Cas
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 3 random cards from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into his or her deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: N
Description: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws a card for each of his or her remaining Prize cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Old Amber Aerodactyl
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Aerodactyl you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Professor Juniper
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Random Receiver
Description: Reveal cards from the top of your deck until you reveal a Supporter card. Put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Twist Mountain
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may flip a coin. If heads, that player puts a Restored Pokémon from his or her hand onto his or her Bench.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 cards, you can’t play this card.) Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Groudon-EX
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If the Defending Pokémon already has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 40 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Darkrai-EX
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [D] Energy attached to it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tornadus-EX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gardevoir
Ability-Effect: Each basic [P] Energy attached to your [P] Pokémon provides [P][P] Energy. You can’t apply more than 1 Psychic Mirage Ability at a time.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Archeops
Ability-Effect: Each play can’t play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve his or her Pokémon.
Effects: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Pokémon Catcher
Description: Switch your opponent’s Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
